plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Trim semiconductor exposure (SMH) to reduce risk.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Add high-quality tech names (MSFT, PANW) for growth.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for about 1.5% per month. It focuses on reducing leverage and concentration, holding more stable assets and cash. Expect lower potential gains but also lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to a small allocation.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Increase position in strong performer (CRWD) for growth.
Add quality tech names (MSFT, PANW) for balanced exposure.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
A moderate cash buffer is maintained for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets about 2.5% per month. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality growth names. Expect moderate gains and moderate drawdowns. It aims for risk-first growth.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) for amplified gains.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Concentrate further in top performers (CRWD) and strong scanner setups (MSFT, PANW).
This plan aims for aggressive growth by leaning into leverage and concentration.
A small cash buffer is maintained.
Monitor for potential short opportunities (SOXS).
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets about 4% per month but carries significantly higher risk. It uses leverage and concentrates in high-conviction names, including leveraged ETFs. Expect amplified gains but also much larger potential drawdowns due to leverage and daily decay of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta single names (CRWD, SMH).
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet concentration limits.
Maximize allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for steady distributions.
Add energy sector ETF (XLE) for income diversification.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income and low drawdowns.
A larger cash buffer is maintained for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets about 1% per month, focusing on capital preservation and regular distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and growth-oriented single names, favoring income-generating ETFs. Expect lower growth potential but also very low drawdowns.
